candidate_check.description: The Ubuntu operating system must generate audit records for privileged activities, nonlocal maintenance, diagnostic sessions and other system-level access.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify the Ubuntu operating system audits activities performed during nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions. 
 
Check the currently configured audit rules with the following command: 
 
$ sudo auditctl -l | grep sudo.log 
 
-w /var/log/sudo.log -p wa -k maintenance 
 
If the command does not return lines that match the example or the lines are commented out, this is a finding. 
 
Note: The "-k" allows for specifying an arbitrary identifier, and the string after it does not need to match the example output above.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure the Ubuntu operating system to audit activities performed during nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions. 
 
Add or update the following rules in the "/etc/audit/rules.d/stig.rules" file: 
 
-w /var/log/sudo.log -p wa -k maintenance 
  
To reload the rules file, issue the following command: 
 
$ sudo augenrules --load
title: The Ubuntu operating system must generate audit records for privileged activities, nonlocal maintenance, diagnostic sessions and other system-level access.
